findings.issue: The P6 confidence reproduction asserts nothing (reports [Pass] regardless), its committed .pr mis-compiled step 1 'compress %original%, write to %archived%' to variable.set (the exact silent intent-drop P6 exists to catch), and confidence is null on all steps in the .pr. A fresh rebuild hard-fails (ValidationErrors / BuilderPlannerFailed) so the documented '⚠ planner VeryLow' warnings do not reproduce at all.
findings.impact: The branch's flagship feature has no honest test. A regression that silently drops a verb keeps this green.
findings.suggestion: Build -> read trace -> assert plan.steps[1].confidence == 'VeryLow' (and compiler pass), or assert the rendered build output contains the warning line. Drive from a fixture/mock plan if the live build can't be deterministic. Stop shipping a variable.set .pr as the unknown-verb reference.

findings.issue: Normalize_PropertyLookupCache_PopulatesOnFirstCall_HitsOnSecond asserts on Tagged.CacheSize, a process-wide static ConcurrentDictionary, while TUnit runs tests in parallel. Passes in isolation, fails in the full suite (a concurrent Normalize adds a (Type,View) entry between the two CacheSize reads). ClearCacheForTests() is itself a global side-effect that can flake neighbouring parallel tests.
findings.impact: Non-deterministic suite; CI flips with thread scheduling; a real cache regression is indistinguishable from this noise.
findings.suggestion: Assert the specific (typeof(Identity),View) key is reused (same reference / no recompute) instead of the global count. If a count assertion is required, mark [NotInParallel] and drop the global ClearCacheForTests() or scope the cache per-test.

findings.issue: Mutation-confirmed: commenting out InvokeGoal's `_executing.Value = true` (the recursion-guard arming) leaves all 7 channel tests green. The guard tests flip the AsyncLocal via reflection, never through a real goal-channel write, so the arm-during-execution path is untested.
findings.impact: If the arming line breaks, a goal-channel writing to its own name stack-overflows in production while the suite stays green — the exact bug this branch's central C# change exists to prevent.
findings.suggestion: Add a test with a real goal body that writes to its own channel name and assert the inner write returns ChannelNotFound (404) rather than recursing.

findings.issue: Fails with 502 Bad Gateway from an external upload endpoint. Not a regression (no HTTP code changed on this branch), but red counts against the run.
findings.impact: Network-dependent test reddens the unit suite when the gateway is down.
findings.suggestion: Mock the transport or quarantine behind an integration tag.

findings.issue: An interactive 'Allow User to execute //nonexistent/ghost-provider.dll? (y/n/a)' prompt leaked to the console during the C# run, waiting on stdin.
findings.impact: Could hang or consume unintended input in a non-interactive CI without a TTY.
findings.suggestion: Supply a redirected input channel (auto-deny) for the test that triggers the permission gate.

summary: Clean rebuild, both suites run. C# 3376/3377 (1 flaky shared-static cache test, passes alone / fails in suite). PLang 233/234 (1 environmental 502). On top of the live failures: the P6 confidence reproduction is a confirmed false green that asserts nothing and ships the silent-verb-drop bug it should catch, and the load-bearing channel recursion guard is untested end-to-end (mutation-confirmed). Verdict FAIL.
